name: Ruin
description: The Scout spots a ruined structure ahead — relic of a vanished age, or more recent loss. Ruins offer shelter, lore, and occasionally something worse.

ancient_watchtower.label: Ancient Watchtower
ancient_watchtower.description: A long-fallen tower from an earlier age — the masonry older than any kingdom the fellowship has heard of.

events.label: The watchtower is sound enough in the base to give shelter for a night. Old carvings worn smooth by weather; a defensible position if trouble finds them.
events.notes: Useful shelter. Possible minor Lore roll to identify the age or origin.

events.label: The watchtower has a reputation — locals have warned of it. At dusk, the air grows colder; at midnight, something walks the broken parapet.
events.on_eye: The haunting has mass, not only presence — a wight or worse.
events.notes: Shadow risk. Success means surviving the night; failure means shadow points or worse.

events.label: The tower's ruins are occupied — a band of orcs, a family of trolls, bandits using it as a base. Approach carefully.
events.on_eye: The occupants have spotted the fellowship first.
events.notes: Likely combat encounter. Scout's stealth may allow bypassing.

events.label: Old arms or supplies hidden in a concealed niche — left by some ranger, long dead. A map, a blade, a ring, a journal.
events.notes: Treasure event. Exact contents at GM discretion — a genuine boon, or a cursed burden.

overgrown_temple.label: Overgrown Temple
overgrown_temple.description: A shrine or temple reclaimed by the wilderness — stones half-swallowed by root and moss, old images weathered past reading.

events.label: A quiet grove grown over a shrine — old stones, a spring still running, a sense of peace that has not been disturbed in centuries.
events.notes: Restorative event. Some traditions say resting here restores Hope; GM discretion.

events.label: The temple has been recently desecrated — symbols defaced, an altar broken. The violence feels fresh; the wrongness lingers.
events.on_eye: The desecrators are nearby, or the shrine's guardian spirit is roused.
events.notes: Atmospheric event. Investigation may reveal perpetrators.

events.label: A solitary figure lives among the stones — a hermit, perhaps mad, perhaps wise, perhaps both. They offer water, or strange counsel, or a question.
events.notes: Encounter event. Roleplay opportunity; may yield lore or a cryptic warning.

events.label: Something guards the temple still — a spirit, a stone watcher, a beast bonded to the place. It is not hostile unless disturbed.
events.on_eye: The guardian's judgement falls against the fellowship.
events.notes: High-variance encounter. Respectful passage is possible; intrusion is not.

abandoned_village.label: Abandoned Village
abandoned_village.description: A settlement gone silent — cottages standing but empty, the wells dry or fouled, the fields returning to wild.

events.label: The village was abandoned recently — within a year, perhaps a season. Furniture still in the houses, tools where they were dropped. What drove them out is not immediately clear.
events.on_eye: Whatever emptied the village is nearby.
events.notes: Investigation opportunity. Shelter available but uneasy.

events.label: A village long gone — roofs fallen, walls standing, bramble through every window. Centuries, perhaps. The name may be lost.
events.notes: Sheltered but atmospheric. Lore roll may yield the village's fate.

events.label: The abandoned village now houses squatters — outlaws, runaways, the desperate. They are wary of travellers; violence possible.
events.on_eye: The squatters are organised and predatory.
events.notes: Social or combat event. Negotiation possible; sharing food earns temporary peace.

events.label: One household remains — an old woman, a small family, a single hermit — the last of those who once lived here. They have stories to tell, if anyone will listen.
events.notes: Roleplay event. Rich in local lore and tragedy.

collapsed_bridge.label: Collapsed Bridge
collapsed_bridge.description: A stone bridge from some earlier realm, now broken — the piers standing, the span fallen.

events.label: The bridge is mostly collapsed, but a narrow course remains — passable with care, dangerous with haste.
events.on_eye: A section gives way mid-crossing — rolls to catch hold, possible lost gear.
events.notes: Athletics/agility event per party member.

events.label: The bridge is impassable, but a rough ford can be found downstream — cold water, uncertain footing, time lost.
events.notes: Minor delay; Explore roll finds the best crossing.

events.label: Something has made a home under the broken bridge — troll, lurker, hungry thing. It has been waiting for travellers.
events.on_eye: The lurker is larger than expected, or not alone.
events.notes: Combat encounter. Stealth may allow avoidance.

events.label: No crossing possible here — the fellowship must detour upstream or downstream, losing a full day or more.
events.notes: Pure time cost. GM decides exact days lost.

ancient_burial.label: Ancient Burial Site
ancient_burial.description: A place of the dead from another age — barrows, dolmens, a circle of standing stones.

events.label: A place of old dignity — a barrow, a ring of stones, wind through the grass. The dead rest quietly here.
events.notes: Atmospheric event. Lore roll may yield historical context.

events.label: The site has been disturbed — a barrow broken open, stones overturned, offerings scattered. Treasure-seekers, or something worse.
events.on_eye: The disturbance has woken something that did not want waking.
events.notes: Shadow risk if the dead are angered. Investigation opportunity.

events.label: The barrow is haunted — a wight or barrow-wraith stirs as the sun touches the stones. The fellowship is seen.
events.on_eye: More than one dead thing walks tonight.
events.notes: Serious shadow encounter. Likely combat and shadow points.

events.label: Someone keeps vigil here — a mourner, a priest, a ranger honouring their ancestors. They are not hostile, but they do not welcome company.
events.notes: Roleplay event. Respectful engagement may yield knowledge or aid.

collapsed_fort.label: Collapsed Fort
collapsed_fort.description: A fortress of some bygone kingdom, now broken — walls tumbled, gatehouse fallen, inner courts open to the sky.

events.label: Enough of the fort remains to shelter the fellowship — a section of wall, a cellar, a guardhouse still roofed. Defensible if needed.
events.notes: Useful shelter; lore roll may identify the fort's origin.

events.label: The ruins are occupied by a neutral party — a wandering tribe, a company of rangers, an order in retreat. They offer shelter, cautiously.
events.notes: Social encounter. A good meeting earns allies or information.

events.label: The fort's ruins are held by a hostile force — orcs, bandits, worse. They use it as a base; the fellowship has been seen.
events.on_eye: The garrison is larger than scouting suggests, or expecting reinforcements.
events.notes: Combat encounter. Stealth or misdirection may allow escape.

events.label: Old bones in the inner court — the fort's last defenders, never buried. Weapons and gear may still be usable; the dead still watch.
events.on_eye: The dead are not content to be looted.
events.notes: Looting opportunity with shadow risk. Respectful passage earns quiet.
